name: Healthcare
tagline: Cut the admin load with agents that keep patient data on-device and on the record.
heroTitle: Reclaim hours from paperwork without exposing patient data.
heroSubtitle: Draft visit summaries, structure intake forms, and answer questions across your own protocols with agents that run on your machine. Patient information stays on-device by default, and every model call is governed and logged.
intro: Clinical and operational staff lose hours to documentation, intake, and chasing information, but patient data can't be pasted into a public chatbot. Ryu runs capable agents locally so sensitive data stays on-device, with the Gateway redacting and logging anything that does leave. It helps a clinician work faster, it never makes the clinical call.

highlights.title: Patient data stays on-device
highlights.description: Run a local model with no telemetry by default, so protected information never leaves the machine unless you allow it.

highlights.title: Governed and logged
highlights.description: Every model and tool call passes through the Gateway with PII and DLP redaction and a full audit trail you can review.

highlights.title: Grounded in your protocols
highlights.description: Spaces and RAG let agents answer from your own clinical guidelines and SOPs, citing the exact source.

highlights.title: A human decides
highlights.description: Ryu drafts and summarizes for review; clinicians make every diagnostic and treatment decision.

useCases.title: Visit documentation
useCases.description: Turn rough consultation notes into a structured summary draft that the clinician edits and signs off.

useCases.title: Intake structuring
useCases.description: Convert a free-text patient intake into clean structured fields, flagging missing information to collect.

useCases.title: Protocol lookup
useCases.description: Answer staff questions from your own clinical guidelines and SOPs, citing the document and section.

useCases.title: Patient communication drafts
useCases.description: Draft plain-language appointment reminders and aftercare instructions in the patient's language for staff to send.

useCases.title: Prior authorization prep
useCases.description: Assemble the supporting details and a draft justification for an authorization request, leaving the submission to staff.

useCases.title: Operations summaries
useCases.description: Summarize scheduling backlogs, no-show patterns, and inventory notes into a daily brief for the practice manager.

examplePrompts: Turn these consultation notes into a structured visit summary draft for me to review and sign.
Convert this patient intake form into structured fields and flag anything missing.
What does our protocol say about post-op wound care follow-up timing? Cite the section.
Draft a plain-language aftercare instruction sheet for a patient recovering from a minor procedure.
Summarize this week's no-show data into a short brief with the top patterns.

faq.q: Is patient data safe with Ryu?
faq.a: Ryu is local-first with no telemetry, so patient information can stay entirely on-device. When you route to a cloud model the Gateway redacts sensitive data on egress and logs the call. This helps you keep data on-device and governed; it is not a regulatory certification.

faq.q: Does Ryu make clinical or diagnostic decisions?
faq.a: No. Ryu drafts documentation, structures data, and looks up your protocols. Every clinical and treatment decision is made by a licensed professional who reviews the output.

faq.q: Can it answer from our own clinical guidelines?
faq.a: Yes. Ground agents on your SOPs and protocols with Spaces and RAG so answers cite the exact source document, and a human verifies before acting.

ctaTitle: Take back the time admin steals.
ctaSubtitle: Document, structure, and look up faster while patient data stays on-device and governed.
ctaNote: On-device by default · PII/DLP redaction · Full audit trail
